message:
Add comprehensive timeline marker tests for state and TUI panels

- Add MarkerType tests for Symbol() and String() methods
- Add unit tests for AddMarker covering:
  - Basic marker creation and retrieval
  - Marker ID generation uniqueness
  - Time-based marker ordering
- Add tests for marker filtering by type and time range
- Add concurrent marker addition safety tests
- Add TUI panel timeline_test.go with rendering tests for:
  - Marker symbols and labels
  - Timeline scrolling behavior
  - Panel layout validation
- Update .beads/issues.jsonl with test coverage tracking

These tests ensure timeline markers correctly represent discrete events
(prompts, completions, errors, start/stop) for the agent dashboard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-168bh","title":"Unit Tests: Watch mode loop and exit conditions","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for watch mode loop and exit conditions.

## Test Cases

### Watch Loop
- Initial assignment pass executes
- Completion detected triggers reassignment
- Unblocked beads detected and assigned
- Poll interval respected

### Exit Conditions
- Ctrl+C (SIGINT) triggers graceful shutdown
- --stop-when-done exits when no beads
- All agents crashed exits with error
- No more idle agents waits

### Streaming Output
- Timestamps accurate
- Assignment events logged
- Completion events logged
- Unblock events logged

### Error Handling
- BV unavailable mid-loop
- Agent Mail unavailable
- Assignment failure recovery

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads to start
- All beads blocked
- Single agent scenario
- Very fast completions

## Logging
- Log each loop iteration
- Log event processing
- Log exit reason

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-16a9","title":"Add built-in prompt library with common patterns (marching-orders, code-review, self-review, cross-review)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:32:23.221603857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:17:06.327311020Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:17:06.327220830Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-16dhv","title":"Use /usr/bin/tmux full path for reliable Enter key sending","description":"## Problem

When running tmux send-keys commands from certain shell environments (particularly when zsh tmux plugin is active), the bare `tmux` command gets intercepted by shell functions/aliases, causing:
- Commands fail silently or produce errors like `_zsh_tmux_plugin_run: command not found`
- Enter key not actually sent to panes
- Users see success messages but agents don't receive input

## Root Cause

The zsh tmux plugin defines functions that wrap tmux, and when running from within tmux (or from scripts sourced in zsh), these can interfere with direct tmux calls.

## Solution

Use the full path `/usr/bin/tmux` (or `$(which tmux)` resolved at startup) for ALL tmux operations throughout ntm, especially:
- `send-keys` commands
- `capture-pane` commands  
- Any command that needs to reliably communicate with tmux

## Files to Update

- All Go files that shell out to tmux
- Ensure tmux binary path is resolved once at startup and used consistently

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All tmux calls use full path (not bare `tmux`)
- [ ] Works correctly in zsh with tmux plugin active
- [ ] Works from within tmux sessions
- [ ] Enter key reliably sent to all panes
- [ ] Add test to verify tmux path resolution","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-22T01:05:24.907919887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:10:12.918102350Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-16ptx","title":"Task: --robot-is-working Command Implementation","description":"## Task: --robot-is-working Command Implementation

### Why This Command Exists

This is the DIRECT ANSWER to the users critical requirement:

> "NEVER interrupt agents doing useful work\!\!\!"

Before ANY restart action, a controller agent must be able to ask: "Is this agent actively working?" This command provides that answer with structured, actionable output.

### Command Specification

```bash
# Check single pane
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2

# Check multiple panes
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2,3,4

# Check all agent panes (excludes pane 1 control shell)
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=all

# With verbosity (include raw sample for debugging)
ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2 --verbose
```

### Output Format (JSON)

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-20T15:30:00Z",
  "query": {
    "panes_requested": [2, 3, 4],
    "lines_captured": 100
  },
  "panes": {
    "2": {
      "agent_type": "cc",
      "is_working": true,
      "is_idle": false,
      "is_rate_limited": false,
      "is_context_low": false,
      "context_remaining": null,
      "confidence": 0.95,
      "indicators": {
        "work": ["```", "Writing to ", "Created "],
        "limit": []
      },
      "recommendation": "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent is actively writing code"
    },
    "3": {
      "agent_type": "cod",
      "is_working": false,
      "is_idle": true,
      "is_rate_limited": false,
      "is_context_low": true,
      "context_remaining": 12.0,
      "confidence": 0.90,
      "indicators": {
        "work": [],
        "limit": []
      },
      "recommendation": "SAFE_TO_RESTART",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent idle with low context (12%)"
    },
    "4": {
      "agent_type": "gmi",
      "is_working": false,
      "is_idle": false,
      "is_rate_limited": true,
      "is_context_low": false,
      "context_remaining": null,
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "indicators": {
        "work": [],
        "limit": ["rate limit", "quota exceeded"]
      },
      "recommendation": "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent hit rate limit"
    }
  },
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 3,
    "working_count": 1,
    "idle_count": 1,
    "rate_limited_count": 1,
    "context_low_count": 1,
    "by_recommendation": {
      "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT": [2],
      "SAFE_TO_RESTART": [3],
      "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT": [4]
    }
  }
}
```

### Recommendation Values and Their Meaning

| Recommendation | Meaning | Controller Action |
|----------------|---------|-------------------|
| `DO_NOT_INTERRUPT` | Agent is actively producing output | Leave alone\! |
| `SAFE_TO_RESTART` | Agent is idle, can be restarted | Safe to send exit + new prompt |
| `CONTEXT_LOW_CONTINUE` | Working but low context | Let finish current task, then restart |
| `RATE_LIMITED_WAIT` | Hit usage limit | Wait for reset or switch account |
| `ERROR_STATE` | Agent in error state | Investigate before action |
| `UNKNOWN` | Could not determine state | Manual inspection needed |

### Implementation Flow

```go
package robot

import (
    "github.com/username/ntm/internal/agent"
)

type IsWorkingResult struct {
    Session   string                     `json:"session"`
    Timestamp string                     `json:"timestamp"`
    Query     IsWorkingQuery             `json:"query"`
    Panes     map[string]PaneWorkStatus  `json:"panes"`
    Summary   IsWorkingSummary           `json:"summary"`
}

type IsWorkingQuery struct {
    PanesRequested []int `json:"panes_requested"`
    LinesCaptured  int   `json:"lines_captured"`
}

type PaneWorkStatus struct {
    AgentType            string            `json:"agent_type"`
    IsWorking            bool              `json:"is_working"`
    IsIdle               bool              `json:"is_idle"`
    IsRateLimited        bool              `json:"is_rate_limited"`
    IsContextLow         bool              `json:"is_context_low"`
    ContextRemaining     *float64          `json:"context_remaining,omitempty"`
    Confidence           float64           `json:"confidence"`
    Indicators           WorkIndicators    `json:"indicators"`
    Recommendation       string            `json:"recommendation"`
    RecommendationReason string            `json:"recommendation_reason"`
    RawSample            string            `json:"raw_sample,omitempty"` // Only with --verbose
}

func IsWorking(session string, panes []int, verbose bool) (*IsWorkingResult, error) {
    result := &IsWorkingResult{
        Session:   session,
        Timestamp: time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
        Query: IsWorkingQuery{
            PanesRequested: panes,
            LinesCaptured:  100, // configurable
        },
        Panes: make(map[string]PaneWorkStatus),
    }
    
    parser := agent.NewParser()
    
    for _, pane := range panes {
        // Step 1: Capture pane output
        output, err := capturePaneOutput(session, pane, 100)
        if err \!= nil {
            // Record error but continue with other panes
            result.Panes[strconv.Itoa(pane)] = PaneWorkStatus{
                Recommendation:       "ERROR_STATE",
                RecommendationReason: fmt.Sprintf("Failed to capture: %v", err),
            }
            continue
        }
        
        // Step 2: Parse with agent parser
        state, err := parser.Parse(output)
        if err \!= nil {
            result.Panes[strconv.Itoa(pane)] = PaneWorkStatus{
                Recommendation:       "UNKNOWN",
                RecommendationReason: fmt.Sprintf("Parse failed: %v", err),
            }
            continue
        }
        
        // Step 3: Build status
        status := PaneWorkStatus{
            AgentType:        string(state.Type),
            IsWorking:        state.IsWorking,
            IsIdle:           state.IsIdle,
            IsRateLimited:    state.IsRateLimited,
            IsContextLow:     state.IsContextLow,
            ContextRemaining: state.ContextRemaining,
            Confidence:       state.Confidence,
            Indicators: WorkIndicators{
                Work:  state.WorkIndicators,
                Limit: state.LimitIndicators,
            },
            Recommendation:       string(state.GetRecommendation()),
            RecommendationReason: getRecommendationReason(state),
        }
        
        if verbose {
            status.RawSample = state.RawSample
        }
        
        result.Panes[strconv.Itoa(pane)] = status
    }
    
    // Step 4: Build summary
    result.Summary = buildSummary(result.Panes)
    
    return result, nil
}

func getRecommendationReason(state *agent.AgentState) string {
    rec := state.GetRecommendation()
    switch rec {
    case agent.RecommendDoNotInterrupt:
        return "Agent is actively producing output"
    case agent.RecommendSafeToRestart:
        return "Agent is idle"
    case agent.RecommendContextLowContinue:
        return fmt.Sprintf("Working but low context (%.0f%%)", *state.ContextRemaining)
    case agent.RecommendRateLimitedWait:
        return "Agent hit rate limit"
    case agent.RecommendErrorState:
        return "Agent in error state"
    default:
        return "Could not determine agent state"
    }
}
```

### Integration with Existing NTM Infrastructure

This command builds on existing robot mode infrastructure:

```go
// Uses existing --robot-tail to capture output
func capturePaneOutput(session string, pane int, lines int) (string, error) {
    // Internally calls same logic as:
    // ntm --robot-tail=session --panes=pane --lines=lines
    return robot.TailPane(session, pane, lines)
}
```

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `cmd/ntm/robot_is_working.go` | CLI flag handling, arg parsing |
| `internal/robot/is_working.go` | Core logic (above) |
| `internal/robot/is_working_test.go` | Unit tests |

### Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | All panes analyzed successfully |
| 1 | Some panes could not be analyzed |
| 2 | Session not found |
| 3 | Invalid arguments |

### Usage Examples for Controllers

```bash
# Controller workflow: Check before restart
result=$(ntm --robot-is-working=myproject --panes=2)
recommendation=$(echo "$result" | jq -r ".panes.\"2\".recommendation")

if [ "$recommendation" = "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT" ]; then
    echo "Agent is working, leaving alone"
elif [ "$recommendation" = "SAFE_TO_RESTART" ]; then
    echo "Safe to restart"
    ntm --robot-smart-restart=myproject --panes=2
elif [ "$recommendation" = "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT" ]; then
    echo "Rate limited, waiting"
    # Could trigger caam account switch here
fi
```

### Why This Must Be Done Before Smart Restart

`--robot-smart-restart` (bd-2c7f4) DEPENDS on this command. The smart restart command calls `--robot-is-working` internally before taking any action. Without this foundation, smart restart cannot be implemented.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Command accepts session and panes arguments
- [ ] Returns structured JSON per specification
- [ ] Correctly identifies working vs idle agents
- [ ] Provides confidence scores
- [ ] Generates actionable recommendations with reasons
- [ ] Exit codes match specification
- [ ] Integrates with parser module (bd-2iw6e)
- [ ] Unit tests cover all agent types and states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:16.367320719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T02:04:26.860294212Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T02:04:26.860232195Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-is-working command with full JSON output including session, query, per-pane status (agent type, working/idle/rate-limited/context-low, confidence, indicators, recommendation), and summary stats. Integrates with agent parser from bd-2iw6e.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16ptx","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:16.393784178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-16ptx","depends_on_id":"bd-2iw6e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:38.851027169Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vc3s","title":"Implement structured error responses","description":"Implement structured error responses for all failure modes.

## Why

All errors must include enough info to diagnose without manual inspection.
Consistent error format enables automated recovery decisions.

## Error Response Schema

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "SOFT_EXIT_FAILED",
    "message": "Agent did not respond to Ctrl-C within 3s",
    "phase": "soft_exit",
    "pane": 4,
    "details": {
      "child_pid": 12345,
      "process_state": "running",
      "last_output": "... truncated ...",
      "attempted_actions": ["ctrl-c-1", "ctrl-c-2", "wait-3s"]
    },
    "recovery_hint": "Try --robot-restart-pane with automatic kill -9 fallback"
  }
}
```

## Error Codes

| Code | Phase | Meaning |
|------|-------|---------|
| PANE_NOT_FOUND | init | Pane doesn't exist |
| SOFT_EXIT_FAILED | soft_exit | Ctrl-C didn't work |
| HARD_KILL_FAILED | hard_kill | kill -9 didn't work |
| SHELL_NOT_RETURNED | post_exit | No shell prompt after exit |
| CC_LAUNCH_FAILED | launch | cc command failed |
| CC_INIT_TIMEOUT | launch | cc didn't initialize |
| BEAD_NOT_FOUND | prompt | Bead ID doesn't exist |
| PROMPT_SEND_FAILED | prompt | Failed to send prompt |

## Required Information per Error

Each error must include:
- error.code: Machine-parseable error type
- error.message: Human-readable description
- error.phase: Which step failed
- error.details: Context for debugging
- error.recovery_hint: Suggested next action

## Logging

Log all errors in structured format for post-mortem analysis:
```json
{"level": "error", "code": "SOFT_EXIT_FAILED", "pane": 4, "timestamp": "..."}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:38:27.569255121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:17:45.172053798Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:17:45.171952998Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vc3s","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:38:27.597236579Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vid","title":"Task: Implement inbox polling in dashboard","description":"## Purpose
Periodically poll Agent Mail inboxes and display pending messages.

## Dashboard Integration
- Poll every 30 seconds for each registered agent
- Display unread count in agent status bar
- Highlight urgent messages
- Support click/key to view message details

## Status Bar Display
cc_1 | Claude/Opus | 34% ctx | ✉ 2 new (1 urgent)

## Polling Logic
func (d *Dashboard) checkAgentInboxes() {
    for paneID, agentName := range d.agentRegistry {
        inbox, _ := d.amClient.FetchInbox(ctx, FetchInboxOptions{
            ProjectKey: d.projectDir,
            AgentName:  agentName,
            Limit:      5,
            UrgentOnly: false,
        })
        
        if len(inbox) > 0 {
            d.pendingMessages[paneID] = inbox
        }
    }
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inbox status shown per agent
- Urgent messages highlighted
- Message preview on demand
- Minimal performance impact

## Technical Notes
Use ticker for periodic polling
Cache results to reduce API calls","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-43oxm","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Probe","description":"Unit tests for Robot Probe command with comprehensive coverage.

## Test Coverage

### Baseline Capture Tests
- Test successful baseline capture
- Test capture with empty pane
- Test capture failure handling (session not found)
- Test content hash generation
- Test baseline comparison (changed vs unchanged)

### Flag Parsing Tests
- Test default flag values
- Test valid method flags (keystroke_echo, interrupt_test)
- Test invalid method flag error
- Test timeout range validation (100-60000)
- Test aggressive flag validation
- Test structured error output for invalid flags

### Keystroke Echo Tests
- Test responsive pane detection
- Test unresponsive pane detection
- Test latency measurement accuracy
- Test content diff detection
- Test null byte sending

### Interrupt Test Tests
- Test responsive detection
- Test interrupt behavior warning in output
- Test unresponsive detection
- Test single Ctrl-C vs double-tap distinction

### Confidence Tests
- Test high confidence: clear response, latency < 1000ms
- Test high confidence: definitely unresponsive (no activity)
- Test medium confidence: slow response
- Test medium confidence: ambiguous change
- Test low confidence: no response but process active
- Test recommendation mapping (healthy, likely_stuck, etc.)

### Timeout Tests
- Test custom timeout handling
- Test timeout expiry behavior
- Test deadline enforcement

### Edge Cases
- Test probe during pane rapid output
- Test probe during ultrathink (long processing)
- Test concurrent probes

## Logging Requirements

All tests must log:
```json
{"test": "keystroke_echo_responsive", "input": {...}, "expected": {...}, "actual": {...}, "passed": true}
```

## Mocking Strategy

- Mock tmux capture-pane output for various states
- Mock send-keys for probe input
- Simulate responsive/unresponsive scenarios
- Mock process activity checks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:33.489747284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:13:08.235424577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:13:08.235281417Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:33.518751555Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-30nv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:54.048701672Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-3ah0k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:57.737414902Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-43oxm","depends_on_id":"bd-x0f5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:34:01.371030414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4gaaz","title":"Task: Define YAML handoff format and types","description":"## Overview
Define the canonical YAML handoff format that will be used across NTM for context preservation.

## Background & Reasoning
From Continuous-Claude-v3: YAML handoffs are ~400 tokens vs ~2000 tokens for markdown. This 80% reduction matters because:
1. Less context overhead when loading handoffs
2. More room for actual work context
3. Parseable by both humans and machines
4. Status line integration requires consistent field names

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/handoff/types.go
```go
package handoff

import "time"

// Version tracks handoff format evolution for backwards compatibility
const HandoffVersion = "1.0"

type Handoff struct {
    // Metadata
    Version   string    `yaml:"version"`   // Format version for migrations
    Session   string    `yaml:"session"`
    Date      string    `yaml:"date"`
    CreatedAt time.Time `yaml:"created_at"` // Precise timestamp
    UpdatedAt time.Time `yaml:"updated_at"` // For edits

    // Status tracking
    Status   string `yaml:"status"`   // complete|partial|blocked
    Outcome  string `yaml:"outcome"`  // SUCCEEDED|PARTIAL_PLUS|PARTIAL_MINUS|FAILED

    // Core fields (REQUIRED for status line)
    Goal string `yaml:"goal"` // What this session accomplished - REQUIRED
    Now  string `yaml:"now"`  // What next session should do first - REQUIRED
    Test string `yaml:"test"` // Command to verify this work

    // Work tracking
    DoneThisSession []TaskRecord `yaml:"done_this_session"`

    // Context for future self
    Blockers  []string          `yaml:"blockers"`
    Questions []string          `yaml:"questions"`
    Decisions map[string]string `yaml:"decisions"`
    Findings  map[string]string `yaml:"findings"`

    // What worked and what didn't
    Worked []string `yaml:"worked"`
    Failed []string `yaml:"failed"`

    // Next steps
    Next []string `yaml:"next"`

    // File tracking
    Files FileChanges `yaml:"files"`

    // Integration fields - populated during recovery
    ActiveBeads      []string `yaml:"active_beads,omitempty"`       // From BV
    AgentMailThreads []string `yaml:"agent_mail_threads,omitempty"` // From Agent Mail
    CMMemories       []string `yaml:"cm_memories,omitempty"`        // From CM

    // Agent info for multi-agent sessions
    AgentID   string `yaml:"agent_id,omitempty"`
    AgentType string `yaml:"agent_type,omitempty"` // cc, cod, gmi
    PaneID    string `yaml:"pane_id,omitempty"`

    // Token context at time of handoff
    TokensUsed int     `yaml:"tokens_used,omitempty"`
    TokensMax  int     `yaml:"tokens_max,omitempty"`
    TokensPct  float64 `yaml:"tokens_pct,omitempty"`
}

type TaskRecord struct {
    Task  string   `yaml:"task"`
    Files []string `yaml:"files"`
}

type FileChanges struct {
    Created  []string `yaml:"created"`
    Modified []string `yaml:"modified"`
    Deleted  []string `yaml:"deleted"` // Track deletions too
}
```

### 2. Create internal/handoff/constants.go
```go
package handoff

const (
    StatusComplete = "complete"
    StatusPartial  = "partial"
    StatusBlocked  = "blocked"

    OutcomeSucceeded    = "SUCCEEDED"
    OutcomePartialPlus  = "PARTIAL_PLUS"
    OutcomePartialMinus = "PARTIAL_MINUS"
    OutcomeFailed       = "FAILED"
)

// Validation error types for detailed logging
type ValidationError struct {
    Field   string
    Message string
    Value   interface{}
}

func (e ValidationError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("validation error: field=%s msg=%s value=%v", e.Field, e.Message, e.Value)
}
```

### 3. Create internal/handoff/validate.go
```go
package handoff

import (
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "regexp"
    "time"
)

var (
    sessionNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`)
    dateRegex        = regexp.MustCompile(`^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$`)
)

// Validate checks handoff for required fields and correct values
// Returns all validation errors (not just first) for comprehensive logging
func (h *Handoff) Validate() []ValidationError {
    var errs []ValidationError

    // Required fields
    if h.Goal == "" {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "goal",
            Message: "required field missing - status line depends on this",
        })
    }
    if h.Now == "" {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "now",
            Message: "required field missing - status line depends on this",
        })
    }

    // Session validation
    if h.Session != "" && h.Session != "general" {
        if !sessionNameRegex.MatchString(h.Session) {
            errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
                Field:   "session",
                Message: "must be alphanumeric with _ or - only",
                Value:   h.Session,
            })
        }
    }

    // Date format validation
    if h.Date != "" && !dateRegex.MatchString(h.Date) {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "date",
            Message: "must be YYYY-MM-DD format",
            Value:   h.Date,
        })
    }

    // Status validation
    validStatuses := map[string]bool{
        StatusComplete: true, StatusPartial: true, StatusBlocked: true, "": true,
    }
    if !validStatuses[h.Status] {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "status",
            Message: "must be complete, partial, or blocked",
            Value:   h.Status,
        })
    }

    // Outcome validation
    validOutcomes := map[string]bool{
        OutcomeSucceeded: true, OutcomePartialPlus: true,
        OutcomePartialMinus: true, OutcomeFailed: true, "": true,
    }
    if !validOutcomes[h.Outcome] {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "outcome",
            Message: "must be SUCCEEDED, PARTIAL_PLUS, PARTIAL_MINUS, or FAILED",
            Value:   h.Outcome,
        })
    }

    // Log all validation errors at DEBUG level for troubleshooting
    for _, err := range errs {
        slog.Debug("handoff validation error",
            "field", err.Field,
            "message", err.Message,
            "value", err.Value,
            "session", h.Session,
        )
    }

    return errs
}

// IsValid returns true if handoff passes validation
func (h *Handoff) IsValid() bool {
    return len(h.Validate()) == 0
}

// SetDefaults populates default values for optional fields
func (h *Handoff) SetDefaults() {
    if h.Version == "" {
        h.Version = HandoffVersion
    }
    if h.Date == "" {
        h.Date = time.Now().Format("2006-01-02")
    }
    if h.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
        h.CreatedAt = time.Now()
    }
    h.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
}
```

### 4. Field Documentation
| Field | Required | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| version | auto | Format version for migrations |
| session | yes | Session identifier |
| date | auto | Date in YYYY-MM-DD |
| created_at | auto | Precise creation timestamp |
| goal | **YES** | What was accomplished - STATUS LINE |
| now | **YES** | What to do next - STATUS LINE |
| test | no | Verification command |
| status | no | complete/partial/blocked |
| outcome | no | SUCCEEDED/PARTIAL_PLUS/etc |
| done_this_session | no | Task records with files |
| blockers | no | Known blockers |
| decisions | no | Key decisions made |
| findings | no | Important discoveries |
| worked | no | What approaches worked |
| failed | no | What approaches failed |
| next | no | Suggested next steps |
| files | no | File change tracking |
| active_beads | no | BV integration |
| agent_mail_threads | no | Agent Mail integration |
| cm_memories | no | CM integration |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Handoff struct defined with all fields including version and timestamps
- [ ] YAML tags match exact field names from spec
- [ ] Constants for valid status/outcome values
- [ ] ValidationError type with structured fields
- [ ] Validate() returns ALL errors (not just first)
- [ ] Validation errors logged at DEBUG level with context
- [ ] SetDefaults() populates version, date, timestamps
- [ ] Integration fields (active_beads, etc.) defined with omitempty
- [ ] Unit tests for serialization/deserialization
- [ ] Unit tests for validation with various invalid inputs
- [ ] Example YAML file in testdata/

## Technical Notes
- Use gopkg.in/yaml.v3 for YAML parsing (already in go.mod)
- Keep field names exactly as specified - status line parser depends on `goal:` and `now:`
- DO NOT rename fields like `session_goal`, `objective`, `focus` - only `goal` and `now` work
- Use log/slog for structured logging
- Version field enables future format migrations without breaking old handoffs

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: All validation errors with field, message, value, session
- DEBUG: SetDefaults called with resulting values
- INFO: Successful validation (for tracing handoff lifecycle)","notes":"Implemented YAML handoff format with types, validation, and comprehensive tests. All 18 tests pass, benchmarks show validation at 437ns/op with zero allocations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:46:38.780059636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:07:34.429069092Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:07:34.427245507Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4gaaz","depends_on_id":"bd-khux0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:50:57.674690210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-p3en","title":"Task: Define command tier taxonomy and categorization","description":"## Overview
Create the foundational tier taxonomy that categorizes all NTM commands into proficiency levels.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go defining:
   - Tier enum (Apprentice=1, Journeyman=2, Master=3)
   - CommandTier struct with {Command, MinTier, Description, Examples}
   - Global registry of all commands with their tier assignments

2. Tier 1 (Apprentice) - Essential workflow:
   - spawn: Create agent sessions
   - send: Send prompts to agents
   - status: View running sessions
   - kill: Stop sessions
   - help: Get help (tier-aware)

3. Tier 2 (Journeyman) - Full standard:
   - All Tier 1 commands
   - dashboard: Interactive TUI
   - context: View context window usage
   - tail: View agent output
   - interrupt: Send Ctrl+C to agents
   - swarm: Swarm orchestration
   - All standard flags

4. Tier 3 (Master) - Advanced/experimental:
   - All Tier 2 commands
   - --robot-* flags for automation
   - Internal debugging commands
   - Experimental features

## Implementation Notes
- Use Go constants for tier values
- Include help text per tier explaining what unlocks next
- Document promotion criteria in tier definitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- taxonomy.go compiles and exports tier constants
- All existing commands have tier assignments
- Tier progression is logical (each tier builds on previous)
- Unit tests verify taxonomy completeness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.875431526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700671521Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700604966Z","close_reason":"Implemented command tier taxonomy in internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go with Tier enum, CommandInfo struct, and complete Registry mapping all commands","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p3en","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.905286067Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pjjk7","title":"EPIC: Canonical Assignment Flow (ntm assign + spawn --assign)","description":"## Goal
Reduce user confusion by providing **one** canonical assignment flow and **one** convenience entry point.

## Decision
- **Keep**: `ntm assign` as the only assignment command.
- **Keep**: `ntm spawn --assign` as the only convenience entry (spawn + assign).
- **Expose**: `--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency|round-robin` on `ntm assign`.
- **Drop/Merge**: `ntm swarm`, `ntm send --distribute`, YAML swarm config, and from‑file assignments.

## Why
Multiple entry points doing the same thing (assign work to agents) multiplies docs, tests, and code paths and will confuse users. The project benefits from a single, well‑designed path.

## Scope
- Keep existing strategies (`balanced`, `speed`, `quality`, `dependency`) intact and documented.
- Add `round‑robin` as an **optional** additional strategy (no renaming of existing ones).
- Implement assignment tracking + reporting so `balanced` can be truly load‑aware.
- Implement `ntm spawn --assign` as a thin wrapper over `ntm assign`.
- Update docs/help to emphasize canonical flow.

## Non‑Goals
- No new orchestration commands outside `assign` + `spawn --assign`.
- No YAML swarm config or additional CLI entry points.

## Success Criteria
- `ntm assign` is the only path for distributing work.
- `ntm spawn --assign` works end‑to‑end with clear output.
- Redundant commands are closed/dropped.
- Strategy list in docs/help matches code.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Currently you need to type the full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching like `vnt destr` as long as it uniquely matches one session.

**Behavior:**
- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options and ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

**Example:**
```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias
```

This would make the CLI much more ergonomic for daily use.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-ptu2","title":"Task: Add event markers and details overlay","description":"## Overview
Add discrete event markers on the timeline with hover/select details.

## Deliverables
1. Event marker types:
   - Prompt sent (▶): When user sends prompt to agent
   - Completion (✓): When agent finishes a task
   - Error (✗): When error detected
   - Start/Stop (◆): Session lifecycle events

2. Marker rendering:
   - Small icons above timeline bar at event time
   - Different colors for different event types
   - Clustered markers when events too close together

3. Details overlay:
   - Show popup when marker selected
   - Include: timestamp, event type, details
   - For prompts: first 50 chars of message
   - For errors: error message

4. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to navigate between markers
   - Enter to expand marker details
   - Esc to close overlay

## Implementation Notes
- Use unicode symbols for markers
- Popup positioning to avoid edge overflow
- Cache marker positions for efficient navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markers appear at correct times
- Different event types visually distinct
- Details overlay shows on selection
- Navigation between markers works
- Unit tests for marker placement","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.496917374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:10:12.468417744Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-3h0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:50.481921380Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.512975659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qjsu","title":"E2E Tests: File reservation with real Agent Mail","description":"## E2E Test: ntm + Agent Mail File Reservation Integration

**Note:** File reservation is implemented in MCP Agent Mail. This tests ntm's integration with that existing system.

### Prerequisites
- MCP Agent Mail server running (`am` health check passes)
- Test workspace with beads

### Test Scenarios

1. **Basic reservation flow**
   - Spawn agent via ntm
   - Assign bead with file paths
   - Verify Agent Mail shows reservation for agent
   - Complete bead
   - Verify reservation released

2. **Conflict detection**
   - Spawn 2 agents
   - Agent 1 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Agent 2 assigned bead touching foo.go
   - Verify ntm reports conflict from Agent Mail
   - Verify holder info shown

3. **Reservation on assign**
   - Run ntm assign with --auto
   - Verify file_reservation_paths called for each assignment
   - Check Agent Mail state reflects reservations

### Verification Commands
```bash
# Check Agent Mail for reservations
# (use Agent Mail MCP tools or am CLI)
```

### Logging
- Log Agent Mail API calls
- Log reservation state at each step
- Log conflict details

   - file_reservation_paths call
   - release_file_reservations call
   - Error handling

- Mock filesystem watcher
- Fixture files for various scenarios

 {"id":"bd-r6d5e","title":"E2E Tests: ntm spawn --assign full workflow","description":"## Test Coverage
Full end-to-end test of ntm spawn --assign convenience flag.

## Test Scenario
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Run ntm spawn --assign (single command)
3. Verify agents spawned
4. Verify beads assigned
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify combined output

## Test Script (tests/e2e/test_spawn_assign.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG=/tmp/ntm-e2e-spawn-assign-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1

echo "[INFO] Starting ntm spawn --assign E2E test at $(date)"

# Setup
echo "[STEP 1] Creating test workspace..."
TEST_DIR=/tmp/ntm-spawn-assign-test-$$
mkdir -p $TEST_DIR
cd $TEST_DIR
br init
for i in {1..4}; do br create "Test bead $i" --type task; done
echo "[OK] Created 4 test beads"

# Spawn + Assign in one command
echo "[STEP 2] Running ntm spawn --assign..."
RESULT=$(ntm spawn ntm-spawn-assign-$$ --cc=2 --no-user --assign --strategy=round-robin --json)
echo "[DEBUG] Result: $RESULT"

# Verify spawn
echo "[STEP 3] Verifying spawn..."
AGENT_COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.spawn.agents | length')
if [ "$AGENT_COUNT" -ne 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected 2 agents, got $AGENT_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] 2 agents spawned"

# Verify assign
echo "[STEP 4] Verifying assignments..."
ASSIGNED=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.assign.assigned | length')
if [ "$ASSIGNED" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected at least 2 assignments, got $ASSIGNED"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] $ASSIGNED beads assigned"

# Cleanup
echo "[STEP 5] Cleaning up..."
ntm kill ntm-spawn-assign-$$
rm -rf $TEST_DIR
echo "[OK] Cleanup complete"

echo "[PASS] ntm spawn --assign E2E test passed at $(date)"
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp each step
- Log combined output
- Log spawn and assign phases separately
- Save full log to /tmp
